exclude_note: The exclude-list rule (-a always,exclude -F msgtype=CWD) SUPPRESSES the CWD sub-record from every audit event group (f3 section 2.3/3.5; CWD ~60 B, f3 section 3.2). Where it overlaps the /etc watch's events, it REDUCES net bytes per event (removes the ~60 B CWD record). It does NOT add volume. Direction suppressive. The watch itself is HIGH (recursive subtree, f3 section 2.2).

rules.note: exclude-list rule; drops CWD records from event groups. SUPPRESSIVE (reduces per-event bytes ~60 B). Contributes 0 additive volume; a naive impl counting it as additive is the trap.
RECURSIVE /etc dir watch (f3 section 2.2), write+attr. HIGH (low1k/typ20k/high200k, f3 section 6). Net per-event bytes REDUCED by the CWD exclude above (900 -> ~840 B).

dominant_tier: HIGH (additive watch), with a NEGATIVE/suppressive exclude reducing per-event bytes
rate_band_basis: f3 section 6: broad dir watch HIGH (recursive). The exclude reduces per-event bytes (~60 B CWD removed), not the event count.
